metadata.text:
<document_metadata>
sourceDocument: .venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pip/_vendor/requests/status_codes.py
published: 4/26/2026, 11:03:35 PM
</document_metadata>

r"""
The ``codes`` object defines a mapping from common names for HTTP statuses
to their numerical codes, accessible either as attributes or as dictionary
items.

Example::

    >>> import requests
    >>> requests.codes['temporary_redirect']
    307
    >>> requests.codes.teapot
    418
    >>> requests.codes['\o/']
    200

Some codes have multiple names, and both upper- and lower-case versions of
the names are allowed. For example, ``codes.ok``, ``codes.OK``, and
``codes.okay`` all correspond to the HTTP status code 200.
"""

from .structures import LookupDict

"resume",
    ),  # "resume" and "resume_incomplete" to be removed in 3.0
    # Client Error.
    400: ("bad_request", "bad"),
    401: ("unauthorized",),
    402: ("payment_required", "payment"),
    403: ("forbidden",),
    404: ("not_found", "-o-"),
    405: ("method_not_allowed", "not_allowed"),
    406: ("not_acceptable",),

    408: ("request_timeout", "timeout"),
    409: ("conflict",),
    410: ("gone",),
    411: ("length_required",),
    412: ("precondition_failed", "precondition"),
    413: ("request_entity_too_large",),
    414: ("request_uri_too_large",),
    415: ("unsupported_media_type", "unsupported_media", "media_type"),
    416: (
        "requested_range_not_satisfiable",
        "requested_range",
        "range_not_satisfiable",
    ),
    417: ("expectation_failed",),
    418: ("im_a_teapot", "teapot", "i_am_a_teapot"),
    421: ("misdirected_request",),

metadata.title: .venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pip/_vendor/requests/status_codes.py
metadata.text:
<document_metadata>
sourceDocument: .venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pip/_vendor/requests/status_codes.py
published: 4/26/2026, 11:03:35 PM
</document_metadata>

codes = LookupDict(name="status_codes")


def _init():
    for code, titles in _codes.items():
        for title in titles:
            setattr(codes, title, code)
            if not title.startswith(("\\", "/")):
                setattr(codes, title.upper(), code)

    def doc(code):
        names = ", ".join(f"``{n}``" for n in _codes[code])
        return "* %d: %s" % (code, names)

    global __doc__
    __doc__ = (
        __doc__ + "\n" + "\n".join(doc(code) for code in sorted(_codes))
        if __doc__ is not None
        else None
    )


_init()
